---
title: Icons
description: The recommended icon library — Tabler Icons. Webfont for vanilla projects, typed React components for the React package.
sidebar:
  order: 4
---

import { Button, Alert, Menu } from "@aortl/admin-react";
import {
  IconHome,
  IconSearch,
  IconSettings,
  IconUser,
  IconTrash,
  IconPencil,
  IconPlus,
  IconChevronDown,
  IconInfoCircle,
  IconCircleCheck,
  IconAlertTriangle,
  IconAlertOctagon,
} from "@tabler/icons-react";

The design system recommends [Tabler Icons](https://tabler.io/icons) — a free, MIT-licensed set of 5000+ outline icons designed for UI. They're a natural fit for admin interfaces: thin, consistent strokes; uniform 24×24 grid; visually quiet enough to sit inside dense forms and tables without competing with content.

Tabler ships in two flavours that map cleanly onto this system: a **webfont** (CSS classes, used in plain HTML via `<i class="ti ti-home"></i>`) and **React components** (`@tabler/icons-react`, fully typed and tree-shakeable).

## Browse and search

The full set is browsable at [tabler.io/icons](https://tabler.io/icons). Each icon page lists its name (e.g. `arrow-right`) in both forms: the CSS class becomes `ti-arrow-right`, the React component becomes `IconArrowRight`.

## Install

Install instructions live next to the rest of the setup for each package:

- **Vanilla CSS** — see [Getting Started › Vanilla CSS](../../getting-started/vanilla/#add-icons-optional) for the CDN `<link>` or `@tabler/icons-webfont` import.
- **React** — see [Getting Started › React](../../getting-started/react/#add-icons-optional) for the `@tabler/icons-react` install.

You can mix both in the same app — vanilla pages use the webfont, React surfaces use the components, and the rendered glyphs are identical.

## Vanilla usage

Drop an `<i>` (or any element) with the class `ti` plus the icon name prefixed with `ti-`:

:::example

```html
<i class="ti ti-home"></i>
<i class="ti ti-search"></i>
<i class="ti ti-settings"></i>
<i class="ti ti-user"></i>
<i class="ti ti-trash"></i>
```

```tsx
<IconHome />
<IconSearch />
<IconSettings />
<IconUser />
<IconTrash />
```

:::

Icons inherit `color` and `font-size` from their parent. Drop one into a button and it picks up the button's text colour automatically.

:::example

```html
<button class="btn btn-primary">
  <i class="ti ti-plus" aria-hidden="true"></i>
  New order
</button>
<button class="btn btn-secondary">
  <i class="ti ti-pencil" aria-hidden="true"></i>
  Edit
</button>
<button class="btn btn-danger">
  <i class="ti ti-trash" aria-hidden="true"></i>
  Delete
</button>
```

```tsx
<Button variant="primary">
  <IconPlus size={16} aria-hidden />
  New order
</Button>
<Button variant="secondary">
  <IconPencil size={16} aria-hidden />
  Edit
</Button>
<Button variant="danger">
  <IconTrash size={16} aria-hidden />
  Delete
</Button>
```

:::

## React usage

Import each icon by name (see the [React getting-started page](../../getting-started/react/#add-icons-optional) for the install step):

```tsx
import { IconHome, IconSearch } from "@tabler/icons-react";
```

Components forward standard SVG props plus a few Tabler-specific ones:

| Prop     | Default        | What it does                       |
| -------- | -------------- | ---------------------------------- |
| `size`   | `24`           | Width and height in pixels.        |
| `stroke` | `2`            | Stroke width (try `1.5` for thin). |
| `color`  | `currentColor` | Stroke colour.                     |

:::example

```tsx
<IconChevronDown size={16} />
<IconChevronDown size={24} />
<IconChevronDown size={32} stroke={1.5} />
```

:::

## Sizing convention

Tabler's natural size is `24`. Inside this design system, smaller looks better — admin chrome leans dense. Recommended sizes:

| Context                                      | Size |
| -------------------------------------------- | ---- |
| Inline next to text (button label, menu row) | `16` |
| Icon-only button or large action             | `20` |
| Dense table rows or compact toolbars         | `14` |

For the webfont, set `font-size` on the `<i>` (or its parent). For React components, pass `size={n}`.

## Accessibility

Icons are usually **decorative** — they sit next to a text label that already conveys the meaning. In that case, hide them from assistive tech:

```html
<button class="btn btn-primary">
  <i class="ti ti-plus" aria-hidden="true"></i>
  Add product
</button>
```

```tsx
<Button>
  <IconPlus aria-hidden />
  Add product
</Button>
```

When an icon is the **only** content (icon-only button, status badge), give the surrounding element an accessible name:

:::example

```html
<button class="btn btn-ghost" aria-label="Delete row">
  <i class="ti ti-trash" aria-hidden="true"></i>
</button>
```

```tsx
<Button variant="ghost" aria-label="Delete row">
  <IconTrash aria-hidden />
</Button>
```

:::

## In context

A few common patterns. See the [Alerts](../../components/alerts/), [Menus](../../components/menus/), and [App Shell](../../modules/app-shell/) pages for more.

### Alert with status icon

The `.alert` class detects a leading `<i>` or `<svg>` and switches to a two-column grid — no wrapper needed, and the icon picks up the variant colour automatically.

:::example

```html
<div class="alert alert-info" role="status">
  <i class="ti ti-info-circle" aria-hidden="true"></i>
  Backups run nightly at 02:00 UTC.
</div>
<div class="alert alert-success" role="status">
  <i class="ti ti-circle-check" aria-hidden="true"></i>
  Changes saved.
</div>
<div class="alert alert-warning" role="alert">
  <i class="ti ti-alert-triangle" aria-hidden="true"></i>
  This action is not reversible.
</div>
<div class="alert alert-danger" role="alert">
  <i class="ti ti-alert-octagon" aria-hidden="true"></i>
  Connection to the database failed.
</div>
```

```tsx
<Alert variant="info">
  <IconInfoCircle size={16} aria-hidden />
  Backups run nightly at 02:00 UTC.
</Alert>
<Alert variant="success">
  <IconCircleCheck size={16} aria-hidden />
  Changes saved.
</Alert>
<Alert variant="warning">
  <IconAlertTriangle size={16} aria-hidden />
  This action is not reversible.
</Alert>
<Alert variant="danger">
  <IconAlertOctagon size={16} aria-hidden />
  Connection to the database failed.
</Alert>
```

:::

### Menu items with leading icons

:::example

```html
<details class="menu">
  <summary class="menu-trigger">Account</summary>
  <div class="menu-popup" role="menu">
    <button class="menu-item" type="button">
      <i class="ti ti-user" aria-hidden="true"></i>
      Profile
    </button>
    <button class="menu-item" type="button">
      <i class="ti ti-settings" aria-hidden="true"></i>
      Settings
    </button>
    <hr class="menu-separator" />
    <button class="menu-item" type="button">
      <i class="ti ti-trash" aria-hidden="true"></i>
      Delete account
    </button>
  </div>
</details>
```

```tsx
<Menu>
  <Menu.Trigger>Account</Menu.Trigger>
  <Menu.Popup>
    <Menu.Item>
      <IconUser size={16} aria-hidden />
      Profile
    </Menu.Item>
    <Menu.Item>
      <IconSettings size={16} aria-hidden />
      Settings
    </Menu.Item>
    <Menu.Separator />
    <Menu.Item>
      <IconTrash size={16} aria-hidden />
      Delete account
    </Menu.Item>
  </Menu.Popup>
</Menu>
```

:::

## Substituting your own set

Nothing in the design system is hard-wired to Tabler. Component icon slots — `<Sidebar.Icon>`, `<Select.Icon>`, `<Select.ItemIndicator>`, the leading `<i>` in `.menu-item` — accept any element. Use Phosphor, Heroicons, your own SVG sprite, or inline SVGs the same way. Tabler is the recommendation, not a requirement.
